commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the intake policy, not a narrow code defect for automation to repair.; Cleared: The diff changes local TypeScript intake classification and focused tests only; it adds no dependencies, workflow permissions, secret handling, package scripts, or downloaded code. (sha=7faebbc25cf8dcdec3366227ffcb54cd96c52a90)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds label-derived metadata signals and a `requires_human` triage path so metadata-only ClawSweeper PR signals are reported without creating repair jobs, with focused repair-intake tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a PR adding a new intake classification path. Source inspection and the contributor's dry-run output cover both intended classification paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the intake policy, not a narrow code defect for automation to repair.

target.title: Separate human-only PR intake signals
target.body:
## Summary

Reintroduce only the focused human-only PR intake classification from #333, rebuilt on current `main` after #335 landed.

This PR classifies metadata-only ClawSweeper signals as `requires_human` instead of enqueueing a repair job, while preserving repair jobs when objective repair blockers exist.

## Scope

```text
- human-only / metadata-only classification only
- no watch-silence state
- no fingerprinting
- no adapter/model code
- preserves author-wide intake from #335
```

## Validation

```text
corepack pnpm run build:repair
node --test test/repair/pr-repair-intake.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
build:repair: passed
pr-repair-intake.test.ts: 5/5 tests passed
```

Covered cases:

```text
- cancelled-only checks stay ignored
- failed checks still write repair jobs
- author-wide discovery still works
- metadata-only ClawSweeper labels route to requires_human
- metadata plus failed check still writes a repair job
```

## Live dry-run note

I attempted live dry-run proof after rebuilding on current main:

```text
node dist/repair/pr-repair-intake.js --repo openclaw/clawsweeper --author Jhacarreiro --limit 100 --dry-run --no-comments
```

Result:

```json
{"status":"ok","repo":"openclaw/clawsweeper","author":"Jhacarreiro","scanned":0,"candidates":0,"requires_human_count":0,"jobs_count":0}
```

I also tried author-wide mode:

```text
node dist/repair/pr-repair-intake.js --author Jhacarreiro --all-open --limit 100 --dry-run --no-comments
```

Result summary:

```json
{"status":"ok","mode":"author-wide","searched":6,"repos_discovered":5,"repos_scanned":0,"scanned":0,"candidates":0}
```

So the branch is mergeable and unit-covered, but the requested live positive proof could not be produced from the currently visible open PR set.
target.author: jhacarreiro
